response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match with 20+ years of professional experience, well above the 6+ year requirement. His current role at Naver directly involves full-stack TypeScript/Node.js work alongside AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which aligns tightly with the job's AI agent development requirement. He has demonstrated proficiency across the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) with cloud and containerization exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker). The main gaps are the absence of explicit NestJS experience, no mention of Vitest or Jest by name (despite clear TDD and testing culture through open-source test tooling), and limited evidence of Kotlin depth. These are learnable gaps for a candidate of this caliber.
strengths: Deep, hands-on TypeScript/Node.js/React experience across multiple production roles including high-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic, millions of mail sends at Tencent)
Current AI-agent and MCP tooling work at Naver is a direct match for the job's AI agent development requirement
Exceptionally strong testing culture evidenced by TDD practice, open-source test runner (Trial) and assertions library (Fluent-Asserts), and community workshops on testing
concerns: NestJS is not mentioned anywhere in the resume despite being the primary backend framework in the job's stack
Kotlin is absent from the resume; Java is listed but with no recent or substantial evidence of use
No explicit mention of authentication, authorization, or security practices, which are called out as required skills
